title: Ensure that Corporate Login Credentials are Used

input_row.title: Ensure that Corporate Login Credentials are Used
input_row.description: Use corporate login credentials instead of consumer accounts, such as Gmail accounts.
input_row.rationale: It is recommended fully-managed corporate Google accounts be used for increased visibility, auditing, and controlling access to Cloud Platform resources. Email accounts based outside of the user's organization, such as consumer accounts, should not be used for business purposes.
input_row.audit: For each Google Cloud Platform project, list the accounts that have been granted access to that project: From Google Cloud CLI gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID Also list the accounts added on each folder: gcloud resource-manager folders get-iam-policy FOLDER_ID And list your organization's IAM policy: gcloud organizations get-iam-policy ORGANIZATION_ID No email accounts outside the organization domain should be granted permissions in the IAM policies. This excludes Google-owned service accounts.
input_row.remediation: Remove all consumer Google accounts from IAM policies. Follow the documentation and setup corporate login accounts. Prevention: To ensure that no email addresses outside the organization can be granted IAM permissions to its Google Cloud projects, folders or organization, turn on the Organization Policy for Domain Restricted Sharing. Learn more at: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/organization-policy/restricting- domains
input_row.impact: There will be increased overhead as maintaining accounts will now be required. For smaller organizations, this will not be an issue, but will balloon with size.
input_row.default_value: By default, no email addresses outside the organization's domain have access to its Google Cloud deployments, but any user email account can be added to the IAM policy for Google Cloud Platform projects, folders, or organizations.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_iam_account_domain_restriction_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program will perform the following checks:

1. Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command `gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID` to retrieve the IAM policies of each Google Cloud Platform project. This command checks which accounts have access and will capture their email domains.
2. Use the command `gcloud resource-manager folders get-iam-policy FOLDER_ID` to list accounts with access at the folder level. This will ensure a broader audit across the organization’s hierarchy.
3. Use the command `gcloud organizations get-iam-policy ORGANIZATION_ID` to check the organizational level IAM policies.

**Specific Configuration Checks:**
- The program will specifically look for accounts included in the IAM policies that do not match the organization’s domain (i.e., any accounts with email addresses that do not end in the organization's specific domain).

**Pass/Fail Criteria:**
- **Pass:** If all accounts listed in IAM policies across projects, folders, and organizations belong to the organization's domain. Example valid domains include `@company.com`, ensuring no external domains, like `@gmail.com`, are present.
- **Fail:** If any accounts from outside the organization’s domain are found in the IAM policies across projects, folders, or organizations.

**Example:** An external account such as `user@gmail.com` or `user@yahoo.com` would indicate a failure in compliance, while an account like `employee@company.com` would be compliant. 

This approach ensures compliance with the control of using corporate login credentials rather than consumer accounts and provides a structured way to determine usability across the GCP environment.
